summary: Shares of chip maker Intel were stalling again Monday after their big run-up over the past month.  Investors are torn between excitement over strong earnings reports from artificial intelligence hyperscalers last week and fears of an AI bubble popping sooner rather than later.  The U.S. announced an effective 10% stake in Intel last August, making it the largest single investor in the U.S.-based chip maker.

content.title: Intel Stock Slips Below Its Record High. Why People Are Worried.
content.summary: Shares of chip maker Intel were stalling again Monday after their big run-up over the past month.  Investors are torn between excitement over strong earnings reports from artificial intelligence hyperscalers last week and fears of an AI bubble popping sooner rather than later.  The U.S. announced an effective 10% stake in Intel last August, making it the largest single investor in the U.S.-based chip maker.

headline: Tesla Semi And Terafab Shift Focus To Vertical Integration And AI
summary: Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA) has begun producing its first high volume Tesla Semi trucks at its Nevada facility. The company is also launching Terafab, an internal semiconductor foundry developed with support from Intel. These projects aim to support Tesla's commercial vehicle, robotics, and autonomous transport ambitions. Tesla sits at the intersection of electric vehicles, energy storage, and software, and the push into heavy trucks and chips adds new dimensions to that mix. The Tesla Semi...

headline: Intel Stock Falls From Its Record High. Why People Are Worried.
summary: The U.S. announced an effective 10% stake in Intel last August, making it the largest single investor in the U.S.-based chip maker.  Since then, the company’s shares are up more than fourfold in large part because Intel makes chips that are used in AI data centers.  “We expect that Intel has higher potential for further upside surprises over the next couple of quarters, given its own in-house foundry capacity,” HSBC analysts wrote Monday.

content.title: Intel Stock Falls From Its Record High. Why People Are Worried.
content.summary: The U.S. announced an effective 10% stake in Intel last August, making it the largest single investor in the U.S.-based chip maker.  Since then, the company’s shares are up more than fourfold in large part because Intel makes chips that are used in AI data centers.  “We expect that Intel has higher potential for further upside surprises over the next couple of quarters, given its own in-house foundry capacity,” HSBC analysts wrote Monday.

headline: Intel appoints Qualcomm executive to lead PC and physical AI business
summary: Intel  said on Monday it had appointed Alex Katouzian to lead the company's ‌PC and physical artificial intelligence unit.  The new role ‌for Katouzian includes the company's crucial PC business, which has ​been one of the mainstays of Intel's revenue and profit for decades.  Katouzian has worked at Qualcomm for more than 20 years and has worked extensively in the ‌company's mobile chip ⁠unit, which makes processors for smartphones and other gadgets.

The new role ‌for Katouzian includes the company's crucial PC business, which has ​been one of the mainstays of Intel's revenue and profit for decades.  Katouzian has worked at Qualcomm for more than 20 years and has worked extensively in the ‌company's mobile chip ⁠unit, which makes processors for smartphones and other gadgets.
